title: Use a repository architecture check and maintainer skill for long-lived AI iteration
context: The product now has enough backend modules, API routes, configuration profiles, and AI automation that periodic narrative-only full reviews are too fragile. AI needs a repeatable way to see coupling drift, route growth, and cold-start expectations before making changes.
decision: Add a machine-readable architecture rule file, an executable ai:architecture report backed by dependency-cruiser and AST probes, an owner/verification inventory baseline, an executable ai:inventory report, and a repository maintainer skill under .ai/skills. ai:doctor and ai:evidence consume these checks so architecture debt, repository surface drift, config drift, and incomplete cold-start context become mandatory gates during routine work.
consequences: Existing architecture debt is accepted only through .ai/architecture-baseline.json entries with owner, rationale, target, and non-expired expiresAt; unbaselined debt, expired entries, PR-added baseline fingerprints, and hotspot growth fail.
New modules, routes, workflows, jobs, storefront entrypoints, site profiles, and AI scripts fail until registered in .ai/inventory-baseline.json with owner and verification commands.
Backend modules must be represented in .ai/system-map.json; newly unmapped modules fail unless explicitly baselined with owner, verification, target, and expiry.
Runtime env reads are scanned with AST support for destructuring, constant computed keys, dynamic access helpers, and registered dynamic key patterns.
Future changes can remove baseline entries as debt is paid down.
AI cold start now includes both machine evidence and task-specific maintainer instructions.
